message:
fix(fr-command): OBJECT ENCODING/REFCOUNT reject trailing extra args [bd-frankenredis-2yc4]

fr-command::object_cmd was using `argv.len() < 3` for the
ENCODING and REFCOUNT subcommand arity checks, so 'OBJECT
ENCODING k k2' silently ignored the trailing k2 instead of
emitting the upstream 'ERR wrong number of arguments for
object|encoding' error. Tightened to `!= 3` to match upstream
object.c::objectCommand semantics. IDLETIME and FREQ already
used `!= 3`.

Also realigns the fr-runtime
latency_graph_without_samples_returns_error test to expect the
new ERR-prefixed wording.

Verified byte-for-byte against vendored Redis 7.2.4: 'OBJECT
ENCODING k k2' and 'OBJECT REFCOUNT k k2' both now return the
upstream wrong-arity error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
